title: crepis glauca var. caulescens
intro: Crepis glauca var. caulescens, also known as the hairy hawk's beard or rough hawk's beard, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. Traditionally, Crepis glauca var. caulescens has been used in herbal medicine, with its roots and leaves possessing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is thought to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, aiding digestion, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Crepis glauca var. caulescens is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
